I'm coming to SF that week to raise our seed round and hire technical talent. A yacht with 150 investors and builders is exactly the room I want to be in.

name: Kai Jet Goh
bio:
As agentic coding increasingly democratises the ability to execute on ideas, the real edge for founders is shifting from technical ability alone to taste, judgment, and the ability to connect insights across domains.

I bring that combination. I’ve built complex distributed systems from scratch at Citadel, won first place in the entertainment track at a major hackathon (YHack) with an educational AI product, and conducted university prize-winning machine learning research. That gives me a perspective across enterprise infrastructure, consumer products, and deep tech, and more importantly a habit of turning ideas into working systems.

I'm confident I would add to the room by bringing both technical depth and broad curiosity as someone who can contribute thoughtful ideas, challenge assumptions, and draw inspiration from other ambitious, high-agency builders at the event.

Here's my LinkedIn if you want to learn more about me :)

https://www.linkedin.com/in/kai-jet-goh-84950528a/

name: Jose Rojas
bio:
AI is gated by two things right now: compute and energy. We go after both, deploying sustainable modular data centers at underutilized power plants and selling that capacity to AI companies at a fraction of hyperscaler pricing.
Backed by AlleyCorp (Kevin Ryan), first site live in Salt Lake with 100% of capacity contracted out, and hundreds more MW coming over the next 6-12 months. One thing I can't say publicly yet: we're closing $1.1B into our data centers in about six weeks.
Everyone on that yacht is building on GPUs. I'm building what they run on.
context: Helios CEO, AlleyCorp/Kevin Ryan backed, modular DC at power plants

bio:
Hi, nice to meet you. I’m Andrea, a rising junior studying CS at Caltech and interning at Fabletics on their database engineering team.

My best friend dropped out to do YC freshman year which catapulted me into the startup world. I was frustrated with the lack of entrepreneurial opportunities on campus and found myself connecting friends to VCs, engineers to founders, and growing the startup community. I now lead the startup club where I host events, connect individuals, and meet like minded innovators across Caltech, UCLA, and USC. I was working on my startup up until last month ($10M valuation) because I realized I enjoy community building and investing more.

You should invite me because you’re going to be hosting the top entrepreneurs and investors. Those founders are going to need engineers, growth, marketers to scale their company, and I can help. If founders are meeting investors and now they have someone to help them scale their vision? You’re going to be known as THE event everyone wished they were invited to.

Feel free to DM me, happy to share more and excited to hear back from you!
context: Caltech CS + Fabletics intern, leads startup club, VC-backed co-founder (departed)

name: Grace Le Cai
bio:
I work at the intersection of computer science, physics, and applied AI. With a BS in CS & Physics and currently finishing my MS in Computer Science, my focus has been on bridging the gap between physical systems and scalable software.

I’d be a strong addition to this group for a few reasons:

Robotics & Simulation Research: I don’t just use existing wrappers; I work on the foundational physics side of autonomous systems. I have first-authored two robotics papers: one on a micro-mobility physical model published at ICRA 2026, and a second on differentiable dynamics recently accepted to IROS 2026.

Production AI Experience: Over two summers at Uber, I worked on their platform engineering teams to deploy generative AI models. I built a multimodal RAG pipeline for image retrieval and implemented Go/Python systems that analyzed multimodal data for privacy risks, which increased recall by 25%.

Autonomous Vehicle Reliability: I’ve moved that mix of simulation and AI into industry. I am currently working on the Autonomous Vehicles team at NVIDIA as a World Model Systems Engineering Intern, focusing on sensor degradation testing for obstacle perception to ensure self-driving stacks remain resilient in unpredictable, real-world conditions.

Building and Leading Teams: I know how to execute outside of a lab setting. As the project lead for Weapon Watch—an AI weapon-detection app for schools—I managed a team of nine engineers and businesspeople, built the PyTorch inference pipeline running on CUDA, and pitched the project to place as a runner-up in UMD's xFoundry competition.
context: UMD CS+Physics MS, incoming NVIDIA AV; ICRA 2026 FRAX, 1st-author robotics - not yet full-time.

name: Rameez Malik
bio:
Yo what’s good! My name is Rameez, I’m 21 years old, and a final-year graduate student at NC State University.

My LinkedIn is essentially my living resume right now and over the past few months, I’ve been documenting my journey as a student builder chasing all things AI, growing to 1.4K+ followers in under 4 months while sharing what I’m learning and building along the way. Some of my most notable accolades are: include 5x hackathon wins (in 4 months), 2x Call of Duty Mobile World Qualifier with a Top 500 NA placement, and single-handedly raising $40k+ for an educational initiative in India that aims to empower 400+ students through scholarship. 

I also have 3+ years of hands-on experience across a variety of surface areas including: hackathons, nonprofits, SMBs, startups, and now enterprise AI through my current internship as an AI/Innovation Developer at Gilead Sciences, where I’m building quality review agents to help accelerate the FDA submission process.

I believe I belong among this curated group of individuals because I bring the perspective of someone who is obsessed with building, hunger (career-wise + food), high-agency, and relevant experience operating across technical, community, and public-facing environments. 

This past semester, I organized a Summer Camp in collaboration with NC State University and we secured a RedBull sponsor (unlimited RedBull for a whole week) and got companies like Nvidia, Lenovo, and ABB onboard for 1:1 mentorship opportunities and fast-tracked internships. 

Beyond my portfolio, I bring fresh energy to every room I’m in. I like connecting with people, making them laugh, good vibes, and turning conversations into genuine relationships. Events like this are valuable because the person standing next to you might become your next investor, cofounder, mentor, customer, or friend and I know I would make the most of that environment.

Not to mention, I have a fire outfit sitting in my closet, and what better place to rock it than a black tie yacht gala in San Francisco haha

And I’ve never been on a yacht before, so the LinkedIn post would go crazy and bring extra visibility to the event itself.

Virtual dap 🤝 and if anyone needs a gym bro after YC Startup School, I’m your guy.
context: Gilead Sciences + stealth AI-native house-reconstruction founder (idea 1w old), 5x hackathon wins 4mo

We're not attending just to network, but to meet founders, researchers, and investors who challenge our thinking, provide candid feedback, and help us build a company that can meaningfully accelerate scientific R&D. We also hope to contribute to the community by sharing our perspective on applying modern Al to one of the most under-digitized sectors: research itself.

Apart from building my own modular solar startup, I work for a specialized firm investing in energy/digital infrastructure companies, backed by a $10B+ family office. We have immense experience in this field, and our investments range from $5M - $250M.

I've been invited by President Biden to speak at the EPA. I also help run Harvard's premier VC group and serve as CIO in Harvard's premier energy group on campus.

I absolutely love meeting other founders working in the energy/climate/AI space. This event would be a fantastic way for me to dive deeper.

name: Lilia Hmadate
bio: We’re building at the intersection of AI and scientific R&D, tackling a problem that’s largely invisible from the outside: most experimental knowledge, especially the failed and unpublished runs, never leaves the lab notebook, so researchers keep repeating the same dead ends. I bring direct experience with this problem from the inside: I worked on CRISPR-based lineage tracing at the Kalhor Lab (Johns Hopkins) and on fluorescence spectroscopy research at Morgan State, so I’ve seen exactly where signal gets lost. I’m coming to pitch, get real feedback from investors and technical builders, and meet people who might either use what we’re building or want to build it with us.

name: Zakir Jiwani
bio:
As a teenager, I helped scale IllicoCash in its early days (the DRC's first bank-led mobile money platform), which has since grown past a million users and $200 million monthly transactions, and is also a Harvard Business School case study.

I'm also a researcher at two labs:
At Penn's quantum lab, I build the FPGA control and verification systems for silicon spin-qubit processors. At Drexel's ICE Lab, I 'm working on developing models for more efficient semiconductor design and manufacturing. 

I've also spent a summer at the best nano materials lab in India at IIT Madras, to co-develop a patent-pending biocompatible protein material for 3D-printed bone and dental implants, that won the Johnson&Johnson Engineering showcase '25.

Outside the lab, I scout for a few early-stage funds and run The Foundry, a 3,000+ member founder community on the East Coast with 50+ active startups in its pipeline.
context: The Foundry cofounder (UPenn); helped scale IllicoCash to 1M+ users / $200M monthly (HBS case); Penn quantum lab researcher

name: Benjamin Chan

I’m 18 and a Carnegie Mellon University dropout. I am the youngest person to ever code for Google (recruited at 16) and I built $40M+ worth of startups before 18. Currently, I’m the Co-founder of Xyla Engineering ($2.5M at a $25M valuation), AI researcher at Elemental AI Labs (empirical eval and mechanistic interprability), Founder/CEO at Aperture AI Labs, and venture partner at Foundry + 996 Ventures. Previously, 2-time startup exits ($15M AI-Native LMS + $3.3M Defense Software sold to Boeing), main lead of $7M SBIR defense contracts at Haylon Tech, and AI/ML research engineer for Google, Boeing, Lockheed Martin, and Northrop Grumman.

I bring an unparalleled defense network and young founder experience/connections. I am looking and will be using the event to invest in promising founders, meet promising investors, and collaborate with fellow AI researchers on future projects.
